title: Role
description:
A Role is the Actor in the Actor > Action > Resource triad — a named job title, organizational role, or permission bundle that performs Capabilities. Roles are referenced by Rules (access), Tasks (assignment), Users (who holds which role), and Processes (operator). A single User may hold many Roles.

Roles unify what were previously two concepts: the organizational job title (what Position used to model) and the access-control grant (what Product Core Role used to model). In practice IAM systems conflate these anyway — an AWS IAM Role like `EC2FullAccess` is both a named bundle of permissions and the thing a principal is assigned. Operational DNA follows the same convention.

name.description: The PascalCase name of the role. Reads naturally as a job title (LoanOfficer, Underwriter) or a permission bundle (Admin, Viewer).

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this role is responsible for or grants.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this role is scoped to.

parent.description: Optional parent Role name — captures org-chart hierarchy (ClosingSpecialist reports to LendingManager) or permission composition (SeniorUnderwriter extends Underwriter). Must reference a declared Role.

examples.name: ClosingSpecialist
examples.description: Closes approved loans and prepares disbursement.

examples.name: Underwriter
examples.description: May approve or reject loan applications.

examples.name: Borrower
examples.description: External borrower — may apply for a loan and view their own records.
